questions.stem: During a PIR, someone says: "This outage happened because Alice pushed wrong YAML. She should have tested it first." What is the BEST blameless response?

options.text: "You're right, Alice should have been more careful."
"This isn't Alice's fault, it's the system's fault."
"Let's discuss what process or validation would have caught this error before production."

questions.stem: Which statement represents a ROOT CAUSE (vs. a proximate cause)?

options.text: "Server had no connectivity because VLAN was wrong."
"VLAN was wrong because operator chose VLAN 1025."
"Operator chose VLAN 1025 because they didn't check existing VPCs."
"VLANNamespace doesn't validate VLAN conflicts at creation time."

questions.stem: Which action item is BEST (most SMART)?

options.text: "Document reserved VLAN ranges (1020-1029) in operator runbook section 'VLAN Selection', including kubectl command. Owner: Alice. Due: Oct 20, 2025."
"Someone should write down VLAN stuff somewhere."

questions.stem: Your team has 5 PIRs this month — all 5 were VLAN-related config errors. What is the BEST next step?

options.text: Accept that VLAN issues are common
Recognize the pattern and prioritize systemic improvement (VLAN validation tooling)
Require operators to re-read VLAN documentation
Add more warning labels to Gitea
